records.summary: London Locks is a well-established East London locksmith with a physical shop in Bow, operating since 2004 with MLA membership. The homepage includes a click-to-call number, service area details, emergency lockout services, and trust signals, indicating a relatively mature digital presence, though with some structural and UX gaps.
records.topOpportunity: No explicit 24/7 hours statement or real-time availability indicator above the fold — a panicked locked-out user cannot instantly confirm someone will answer right now, which a conversion-optimised redesign with a sticky emergency CTA bar could solve.
records.notes: A solid lead for upselling schema markup, Google Reviews integration, and mobile page speed optimisation rather than a full rebuild — MLA badge and no-call-out-fee messaging are strong but buried too far down the page.
